expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Module name is meaningless (utils.py)' calls it a junk drawer / dumping-ground anti-pattern.

expectations.text: problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
expectations.evidence: Section 2 explicitly lists Manager among the generic non-descriptive identifiers.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Metaprogramming via __getattr__' explains the silent no-op dispatch problem.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 4 'Dangerous dynamic execution (eval)' flags it as an RCE vector opaque to static analysis.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Deep, content-free inheritance chain' describes Base -> Entity -> Persisted -> Auditable -> User as vestigial taxonomy.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'No type annotations on public API' enumerates untyped functions and return types.

expectations.text: refactored.py's public functions have type annotations on parameters and return values
expectations.evidence: identity(value: T) -> T and add_integers(left: int, right: int) -> int both fully typed.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: No eval or exec calls in refactored.py.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: User is a flat @dataclass with no inheritance chain (depth 1 from object).

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Rationale cites agents reasoning statically, LLM tooling, agents predicting behavior, and enumerates agent capabilities gained.

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
refactored.py uses @dataclass(slots=True) or @dataclass(frozen=True) to prevent ad-hoc attribute fabrication (v1.3 guidance)
rationale.md or problems.md explains why slots/__all__ matters for agents (hallucinated attributes, import of internal helpers) — the v1.3 framing
expectations.evidence: Rationale mentions __all__ makes public surface explicit and frozen=True prevents mutation, but does not frame it as preventing agent hallucinated-attribute or internal-helper-import failure modes.
